defs: A pin set on the face of a dial, to cast a shadow; a style. See Style.

quotes: May I not write in such a stile as this?

records.ety: [OE. stile, AS. stigel a step, a ladder, from stīgan to ascend; akin to OHG. stigila a stile. √164. See Sty, v. i., and cf. Stair.]

defs: A step, or set of steps, for ascending and descending, in passing a fence or wall.
quotes: There comes my master . . . over the stile, this way.
Over this stile in the way to Doubting Castle.

defs: One of the upright pieces in a frame; one of the primary members of a frame, into which the secondary members are mortised.
notes: ☞ In an ordinary door the principal upright pieces are called stiles, the subordinate upright pieces mullions, and the crosspieces rails. In wainscoting the principal pieces are sometimes called stiles, even when horizontal.
